question: Implement a `ResourceHandler` class that allocates dynamic memory for an integer array of a given size in its constructor. Implement a destructor to correctly deallocate this memory and print a message indicating destruction.

test_cases.expected:
ResourceHandler (ID 1) created for array of size 3. Memory allocated.
ID 1 Data: [0, 0, 0]
ResourceHandler (ID 2) copy constructed from ID 1 for array of size 3. Memory allocated.
ID 2 Data: [10, 11, 0]
ResourceHandler (ID 3) created for array of size 5. Memory allocated.
ID 3 Data: [0, 0, 0, 0, 0]
ResourceHandler (ID 3) move assigned from ID 1 (now empty).
ID 3 Data: [10, 0, 0]
ResourceHandler (ID 4) created for array of size 2. Memory allocated.
ResourceHandler (ID 5) move constructed from ID 4 (now empty).
ID 5 Data: [0, 0]
ResourceHandler (ID 5) for array of size 2 destroyed. Memory deallocated.
ResourceHandler (ID 4) for array of size 0 destroyed. Memory deallocated.
ResourceHandler (ID 3) for array of size 3 destroyed. Memory deallocated.
ResourceHandler (ID 2) for array of size 3 destroyed. Memory deallocated.
ResourceHandler (ID 1) for array of size 0 destroyed. Memory deallocated.

ResourceHandler (ID 1) created for array of size 0. No memory allocated.
ID 1 Data: (empty/size 0)
ResourceHandler (ID 2) copy constructed from ID 1 for array of size 0. No memory allocated.
ID 2 Data: (empty/size 0)
ResourceHandler (ID 3) created for array of size 5. Memory allocated.
ID 3 Data: [0, 0, 0, 0, 0]
ResourceHandler (ID 3) move assigned from ID 1 (now empty).
ID 3 Data: (empty/size 0)
ResourceHandler (ID 4) created for array of size 2. Memory allocated.
ResourceHandler (ID 5) move constructed from ID 4 (now empty).
ID 5 Data: [0, 0]
ResourceHandler (ID 5) for array of size 2 destroyed. Memory deallocated.
ResourceHandler (ID 4) for array of size 0 destroyed. Memory deallocated.
ResourceHandler (ID 3) for array of size 0 destroyed. Memory deallocated.
ResourceHandler (ID 2) for array of size 0 destroyed. Memory deallocated.
ResourceHandler (ID 1) for array of size 0 destroyed. Memory deallocated.
